brave_results.description: David Hays, a Broadway set and lighting designer and founding artistic director of the National Theater of the Deaf, died Tuesday, February 17, at 95.
brave_results.title: David Hays Dies: Broadway Designer, National Theater Of The Deaf Co-Founder Was 95

brave_results.description: He created sets and lighting for dozens of productions, including “Long Day’s Journey Into Night,” and established a new art form with his theater of the deaf, combining sign and spoken language.
brave_results.title: David Hays, 95, Dies; Top Broadway Designer and Theater of Deaf Founder - The New York Times
